tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58553,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Update design.md for reachability-based model filtering and shared resource model handling","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** [REDACTED] (PR #58482) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #58482 introduced two significant behavioral changes to the provisioning generator: 1. **Reachability-based model/enum filtering** — `ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks resource models' property graphs recursively (`CollectReachableTypes()`) and only passes reachable `InputModelType`/`InputEnumType` instances to the type factory. This replaces the old approach of emitting all input models/enums and relying on the post-processor to prune unreferenced types. 2. **Shared resource model handling** — `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore` now handles the case where one `InputModelType` backs multiple `ProvisioningResourceProvider` instances. It selects the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name (alphabetically first as a tiebreaker), ensuring flattened properties are forwarded correctly. ## Gaps Found - **`design.md` § 6 "Output Library"** currently says: *"Only resources are marked as 'types to keep' — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums."* This is no longer accurate. The filtering now happens up-front via `CollectReachableTypes()` in `ProvisioningOutputLibrary`, not via post-processor pruning. The section must be updated to describe the reachability walk (base/derived models, properties, array/dictionary/union element types) and explain why it is needed (avoid dead types like `XxxListResult` paging envelopes, patch wrappers, and error models). - **`design.md` § 5 "TypeFactory & Type Resolution"** (or § 6 "Resource Provider") has no mention of the shared resource model scenario where a single `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider`. The canonical provider selection logic and why it is necessary (so `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` on the correct parent resource) is undocumented. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` **Action:** Edit **Change 1 — Replace the "Output Library" paragraph (§ 6)** Locate this text: ```` The output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead iterates input models and enums directly, routing each through our TypeFactory. Only resources are marked as "types to keep" — the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums. ``` Replace with: ``` The output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead builds the set of models and enums to emit explicitly via a **reachability walk** (`CollectReachableTypes`). Starting from every resource model registered in `ArmProviderSchema.Resources`, it recursively traverses base/derived types, all property types, and element types of array, dictionary, and union types. Only models and enums reachable through this walk are passed to the type factory. This avoids emitting dead types that have no place in a provisioning library — for example, paging envelope types (`XxxListResult`), patch/update wrappers, and generic error models. These types appear in the full input model graph but are never referenced from a resource's property surface. ```` **Change 2 — Add a "Shared Resource Models" subsection** In § 5 "TypeFactory & Type Resolution", after the existing "Resource Detection" subsection, add: ```markdown ### Shared Resource Models In rare cases the same `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider` (for example, when `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` creates a virtual sub-resource whose payload reuses the parent resource's model). `CreateModelCore` must return a single `ModelProvider` for each input model. When the output library records multiple providers for one model, the type factory selects the **canonical provider** — the one whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name. If no exact match exists, it falls back to the alphabetically first resource name. This ensures the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` attaches flattened forwarding properties to the parent resource (e.g., `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` / `SkuName`), while the sub-resource (e.g., `ProfileRevision`) only exposes the properties it needs. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippets are required for this change — the affected file is an internal design document, not a user-facing README. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The file being updated (`docs/design.md`) is an internal design document, not a user-facing package README. Standard README section validation does not apply. ### Step 4: Validate This is a documentation-only change (Markdown). No build or test steps are required. After editing: 1. Confirm the Markdown renders correctly (no broken headings, fenced blocks, or list formatting). 2. Optionally run a Markdown linter if one is configured for the repo. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24819209510/agentic_workflow) · ● 933.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58553","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T05:57:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:57:10Z"}

If no exact match exists, it falls back to the alphabetically first resource name. {"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. It would make the "upserting" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property alone.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"goodcoffeecode","id":1698190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTgxOTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1698190?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/goodcoffeecode","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. {"total_count":24,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4222840604,"number":57921,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Commit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now: - **If the default API version is GA** → only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded) - **If the default API version is itself a preview** → all versions (GA + preview) are listed The generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior. ## Gaps Found - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` — The "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section says *"Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order"* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview. - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` — Section 6 "Output Types" describes `ResourceVersions` only as *"nested class with GA API version constants"*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included. - Neither document explains what constitutes a "preview" version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic — version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.).
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` Edit the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section. Locate the paragraph: ```markdown Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order: ``` Replace it with: ```markdown Lists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource: - **When the default version is GA** — only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out. - **When the default version is itself a preview** — all versions (GA and preview) are included. A version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive). ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` In Section 6 "Output Types" → "Resource Provider" bullet list, locate: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants ``` Replace with: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain. ### Step 3: Verify README structure These are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check). No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generator] ARM resource common properties should be emitted before other properties","body":"## Description The new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator emits ARM resource properties in a different order than the old reflection-based generator. Specifically, `properties` is emitted before `name` and `location`, which changes the Bicep output ordering. ### Expected behavior The old reflection-based generator sorted properties with this ordering logic (from `Specification.Analyze.cs`): ```csharp resource.Properties.OrderBy(p => (p.Name == "Name" ? 0 : p.IsReadOnly ? '3' : p.IsRequired ? '1' : '2') + p.Name) ``` This ensured `name` was always first, followed by required properties alphabetically, then read-only/output properties. Generated `DefineProvisionableProperties()`: ```csharp _name = DefineProperty("Name", ["name"], isRequired: true); _location = DefineProperty("Location", ["location"], isRequired: true); _properties = DefineModelProperty("Properties", ["properties"], isRequired: true); ``` ### Actual behavior The new generator emits: ```csharp _properties = DefineModelProperty(nameof(Properties), new string[] { "properties" }, isRequired: true); _name = DefineProperty(nameof(Name), new string[] { "name" }, isRequired: true); _location = DefineProperty(nameof(Location), new string[] { "location" }, isRequired: true); ``` Since the Bicep emitter uses insertion order, this causes `properties:` to appear before `name:` in the Bicep output. ### Impact All regenerated provisioning libraries will have a different Bicep property ordering than before. While functionally equivalent, this is a behavioral change that breaks existing tests and differs from standard Bicep conventions where `name` and `location` typically appear before `properties`. ### Proposed fix The provisioning generator should ensure ARM resource common properties (`name`, `location`, `tags`) are always defined before the `properties` bag and other custom properties in `DefineProvisionableProperties()`.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues generation","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNTA4NDExMTc3"},{"id":6560170331,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Provisioning","name":"Provisioning","color":"ffeb77","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABhwRBWw"}],"comments":0,"closed_at":"2026-03-27T08:18:56Z","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:01:06Z","updated_at":"2026-03-27T08:18:56Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57489/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM72vP_v"},{"id":4139580056,"number":57488,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Child resource location property incorrectly marked as required instead of output-only","body":"## Description The provisioning generator incorrectly marks `location` as `isRequired: true` for child resources (e.g., `KeyVaultSecret`) when the TypeSpec definition has `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` (read-only) and is optional. ### Expected behavior For `Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets`, the TypeSpec definition is: ```typespec @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) location?: string; ``` The old reflection-based generator correctly generated: ```csharp _location = DefineProperty("Location", ["location"], isOutput: true); ``` ### Actual behavior The new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator generates: ```csharp _location = DefineProperty(nameof(Location), new string[] { "location" }, isRequired: true); ``` This causes `location: location` to appear in the Bicep output for child resources, which is incorrect — the [Bicep reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/templates/microsoft.keyvault/vaults/secrets?pivots=deployment-language-bicep) confirms `location` is not a settable property on `vaults/secrets`. ### TypeSpec reference https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/[REDACTED]/specification/keyvault/resource-manager/Microsoft.KeyVault/KeyVault/Secret.tsp ### Impact Any provisioning library regenerated with the new generator will emit spurious `location` properties for child resources.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1508411177,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/CodeGen","name":"CodeGen","color":"e99695","description":"Issues Milestone: Migrate existing provisioning libraries to TypeSpec-based generator","body":"## Overview Tracking the migration of all existing provisioning libraries from the reflection-based generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`). Only provisioning libraries whose peer management library has already been migrated to TypeSpec (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) are eligible for migration. Additionally, the provisioning generator targets **stable (GA) API versions only**, so libraries whose TypeSpec spec only defines preview versions are blocked until a stable version is added. ## API Version Status | Library | Mgmt API Version | Spec Has Stable? | Status | |---|---|---|---| | Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault | `2026-02-01` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** | | Azure.Provisioning.Communication | `2026-03-18` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** | | Azure.Provisioning.Storage | `2025-08-01` | :white_check_mark: Yes | **Ready** | | Azure.Provisioning.AppConfiguration | `2025-06-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.ContainerRegistry | `2026-01-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.EventHubs | `2025-05-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.Kubernetes | `2025-12-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.RedisEnterprise | `2025-08-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.Search | `2026-03-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.ServiceBus | `2025-05-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.SignalR | `2025-01-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | | Azure.Provisioning.WebPubSub | `2025-08-01-preview` | :x: No | Blocked | ## Sub-issues - [ ] #56820 - [ ] #57382 - [x] #57383 - [ ] #56747 - [ ] #57384 - [ ] #56821 - [ ] #57385 - [ ] #56822 - [ ] #57386 ## Shared Generator Issues These generator issues affect multiple provisioning libraries and must be resolved to unblock migrations: - #56751 - Mgmt `NameVisitor` property renames not applied - #56752 - Missing `[DataMember]` attribute on enum values - #56744 - Generate BuiltInRole types and CreateRoleAssignment methods - #56753 - Generate custom action methods (e.g., GetKeys) - #56745 - `ResourceVersions` should include all historical GA API versions - #56743 - Missing `GetResourceNameRequirements()` override ## Related - Provisioning generator tracking: #56738 - Provisioning generator PR: {"number":57921,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** commit 21456f8 (#57862) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Commit 21456f8 added conditional preview API version filtering in `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`. Previously, `ResourceVersions` may have included preview versions even when the default was GA. Now: - **If the default API version is GA** → only GA versions are listed in `ResourceVersions` (previews are excluded) - **If the default API version is itself a preview** → all versions (GA + preview) are listed The generated test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this new filtering, and `sdk/batch/Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml` was updated to point to the commit that introduced this behavior. ## Gaps Found - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` — The "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section says *"Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order"* without explaining that preview versions **are** included when the default version is itself a preview. - `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` — Section 6 "Output Types" describes `ResourceVersions` only as *"nested class with GA API version constants"*, omitting the conditional logic that governs which versions are included. - Neither document explains what constitutes a "preview" version (i.e., the `IsPreviewApiVersion` heuristic — version strings containing `preview`, `alpha`, `beta`, etc.). 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` Edit the "ResourceVersions Nested Class" section. Locate the paragraph: ```markdown Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order: ``` Replace it with: ```markdown Lists supported API versions in reverse chronological order. The set of included versions depends on the default (latest) API version for the resource: - **When the default version is GA** — only GA versions are included; preview versions are filtered out. - **When the default version is itself a preview** — all versions (GA and preview) are included. A version is considered a preview if its string contains `preview`, `alpha`, or `beta` (case-insensitive). ``` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` In Section 6 "Output Types" → "Resource Provider" bullet list, locate: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants ``` Replace with: ```markdown - **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with API version constants. When the default (latest) API version is GA, only GA versions are included (previews are filtered out). When the default is itself a preview, all versions (GA + preview) are included. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No snippet changes required — these are design/spec documents, not README files using the snippet toolchain. ### Step 3: Verify README structure These are internal design documents (`docs/design.md`, `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md`) for a generator package, not a user-facing SDK README. No standard section structure check applies. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands from the repo root. Only the first command applies since there are no C# or test file changes: 1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/generator/` — confirm generator still builds cleanly after doc edits (no code changes, so this is a sanity check). No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24122942156/agentic_workflow) · ● 961.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57921","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","labels":["CodeGen","Provisioning","bot"],"assignees":["ArthurMa1978"],"comments":2,"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T15:30:32Z"}

No snippet update or API export script is needed for documentation-only edits. ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24122942156/agentic_workflow) · ● 961.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57921","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","labels":["CodeGen","Provisioning","bot"],"assignees":["ArthurMa1978"],"comments":2,"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T07:28:06Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T15:30:32Z"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates to `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` to reflect two behavioral changes introduced in PR #58482: reachability-based model/enum filtering via `CollectReachableTypes()` in `ProvisioningOutputLibrary`, and shared resource model handling logic in `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore`.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (Provisioning generator)
**Affected API:** `ProvisioningOutputLibrary.CollectReachableTypes()`, `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore`
**Scenarios:**
- Update `design.md` § 6 "Output Library" to replace the now-inaccurate statement about post-processor pruning with a description of the reachability walk
- Add a "Shared Resource Models" subsection to `design.md` § 5 "TypeFactory & Type Resolution" documenting canonical provider selection logic

**Root ask:** Keep the internal design document accurate following the behavioral changes in PR #58482.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only task — no runtime bug is present. The changes are straightforward Markdown edits to an internal design document.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58482 (commit [REDACTED]) to confirm the behavioral changes match the described intent
2. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` per the Implementation Guide in the issue body
3. Verify Markdown renders correctly (headings, fenced blocks, list formatting intact)
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Provisioning` — the issue is explicitly about the Provisioning generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`), which generates `Azure.Provisioning.*` libraries
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — code generation issues in `/eng/packages/` are labeled "CodeGen" (color #e99695); the issue title "[Provisioning Generator]" and service directory confirm this
- **Confidence:** High — matches the pattern of several quality reference issues (#57921, #57489, #57488, #57387, #57205) all labeled `Provisioning` + `CodeGen`
</details>

<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>
